sections.subtitle: Four ecosystems and three villages
sections.text: Alpine lake, biodiverse wetland, vast grassland, ancient forest and hidden minority villages. Join our conservation and sustainability expert Lukas and a local Yi minority guide to experience and understand the rich nature and culture of this rich valley. You'll leave knowing four local ecosystems and two ethnic groups.

items.title: Lashi Wetland Park
items.subtitle: Lashi Wetland Park
items.text: Walk the high-altitude wetland at 2,440m, where 235 bird species — including 7 nationally-protected kinds — feed, rest, and breed. Stand on the boardwalk and hear the wind comb through the reeds.

items.title: Wildlife Rescue Station
items.subtitle: Lashi Wetland Conservation Bureau
items.text: Gain exclusive insights into wetland protection at the Conservation Bureau. Meet with conservationists, learn about their work, and understand the challenges and strategies involved in preserving this crucial ecosystem.

image.altText: Lashi Wetland Conservation Bureau & Wildlife Rescue Station

items.title: Bird Survey
items.subtitle: Lashi Wetland Nature Reserve
items.text: Pick up binoculars and a bird manual to read the wetland by its birds: a great cormorant fishing alone, a flock of bar-headed geese commuting between lake and field, a pair of crested grebes in their spectacular mating dance.

items.title: Dayu Naxi Village
items.subtitle: Dayu Naxi Village
items.text: Walk around the picturesque village, enjoy traditional lunch where locals gather for the best cured meats, Tibetan barley bread, seasonal vegetables and yak butter tea. Sip on local coffee enjoying views of the lake.

image.altText: Local coffee with views of grassland, wetland and lake at Dayu village

items.title: Luoma Cliffs
items.subtitle: Luoma Cliffs
items.text: Undertake a short but memorable trek through the forests to the dramatic Luoma Cliffs, where sweeping views of the surrounding landscape await, enriched by insights into local forest biodiversity and conservation.

items.title: Boduoluo Yi Village
items.subtitle: Boduoluo Yi Minority Village
items.text: End the day in Boduoluo, a Yi minority village tucked into the hills where the Tea-Horse Road once climbed from Southeast Asia to Tibet. Witness the daily life and customs of the unique Yi ethnic group.

items.title: Ancient Forest Walk
items.subtitle: Headwater Forest
items.text: Step into the original forest that crowns the ridges above Lashi — spot rare alpine plants and mushrooms, listen for rare birds, and feel the quiet of a forest the village has protected for generations.

image.altText: Mossy old-growth forest above Lashi Wetland

items.title: Sustainability in Action
items.subtitle: Community Conservation
items.text: Gain an exclusive insight into the Lashi Community Conservation Project — the village-led program protecting wetland, watershed forest, and old water-source rules. Your visit helps the project to live on.

items.title: Nanyao Naxi Village
items.subtitle: Nanyao Village
items.text: Conclude your day with a visit to Nanyao Yi minority village and enjoy a traditional dinner of Huotang – a mix of hotpot and teppanyaki. Experience the Naxi hospitality and join a bonfire dance with the locals.

image.altText: Traditional Nanyao Naxi village house with tiered roof

activities: Begin your day with a pickup from your Lijiang hotel, then head to Lashi Wetland Park to explore the heart of its ecosystem. Learn about the delicate balance of flora and fauna, then visit the Lashi Wetland Conservation Bureau for exclusive insights into ongoing preservation projects. After the bureau, continue to the Lashi Wetland Nature Reserve to discover its rich wildlife and bird species.

After the morning at the Wetland Park, Conservation Bureau, and Nature Reserve, drive to Dayu Naxi Village for a traditional lunch with locals — cured meats, Tibetan barley bread, and yak butter tea — paired with locally roasted coffee and views of grassland, wetland, and lake. Then take a short trek to Luoma Cliffs for sweeping views of the valley, before stepping into the original headwater forest that feeds Lashi. End the afternoon in Boduoluo Yi village, tucked into the hills where the Tea-Horse Road once climbed from Southeast Asia to Tibet — witness the daily life and customs of the unique Yi ethnic group.

Conclude your day with a visit to Nanyao Yi minority village, where you sit down to a traditional dinner of Huotang — a mix of hotpot and teppanyaki — and join a bonfire dance with the locals. After dinner, Lukas will lead a final discussion on the Lashi Community Conservation Project — the village-led program protecting wetland, watershed forest, and old water-source rules that your visit has helped sustain. Then return to your Lijiang hotel.

contactNote.text: For groups of 1 person or more than 6 people

included.text: English-Speaking Guide (Lynne Lyu or Lukasz Madrzynski), Local Guide, Traditional Local Lunch and Dinner, Private Transportation, Entrance Tickets, Binoculars for Birdwatching, Outdoor Travel Insurance.
included.notIncluded: Personal Expenses (Souvenirs, etc.), Voluntary Gratuities for the Guides
included.meetingPoint: Pickup: 9:00 AM at your Lijiang hotel | Return: Approximately 8:00 PM to your Lijiang hotel

sections.subtitle: Click on a question to reveal the answer

items.question: What should I bring?
items.answer: We recommend comfortable hiking shoes, layers for changing temperatures, raincoat or umbrella for rainy days, sun protection, a water bottle and your camera.

items.question: Is this suitable for beginners?
items.answer: Yes! This is a mild-level tour suitable for all fitness levels. You'll walk 5-6 km on gentle trails at altitudes of 2,400-3,250m.

items.question: What's the cancellation policy?
items.answer: Full refund if cancelled 7+ days before the tour. 50% refund for 3-6 days. No refund within 48 hours. Weather contingencies always have alternatives.

items.question: What happens if it rains?
items.answer: Most activities continue in light rain. For safety during heavy storms, we'll adjust the itinerary to indoor cultural experiences and conservation center visits.

items.question: Are there any physical requirements?
items.answer: Basic fitness is recommended. The hiking distance is short (5-6 km) on generally gentle terrain at altitude.

items.question: How do I get to the meeting point?
items.answer: We provide pickup from any hotel in Lijiang Old Town area. Contact us to arrange an alternative meeting point if needed.

items.question: What language do the guides speak?
items.answer: We are fluent in English, Polish and Chinese.

items.question: How does this trip support conservation?
items.answer: Each booking directly supports local conservation efforts and community development. We partner with wetland conservation bureaus, employ local Naxi and Yi guides, and contribute to local environmental protection initiatives. Your bird survey counts feed the reserve's biodiversity database. Our small-group format (max 6) minimizes impact on these fragile ecosystems while maximizing the benefit to the people protecting it.

title.text: Ready to Visit the Heavenly Valley?

seo.metaTitle: Lijiang Heavenly Valley Tour | Full-Day Yunnan Alpine Ecotour
seo.metaDescription: Experience alpine wetland, grassland and forest ecosystems on this full-day Yunnan ecotour in Lijiang Heavenly Valley. Explore biodiversity with a local guide. Small-group Yunnan eco tour.
